title: Data Literacy for AI

modules.title: Data Quality and Preparation
modules.sub: Why clean, accurate data is the foundation of reliable AI
modules.description: Learners examine what makes data high or low quality and why it matters for AI outputs. They work through common data problems—such as missing values, bias, and inconsistency—and explore how preparation steps shape what an AI system can and cannot do.

modules.title: Understanding Training Data
modules.sub: How the data AI learns from shapes its behaviour and limits
modules.description: Learners investigate how training datasets are constructed and why their composition directly influences an AI model's capabilities and blind spots. They analyse examples of models that learned from skewed or unrepresentative data and consider the downstream consequences for real-world decisions.

modules.title: Data Sources and Collection
modules.sub: Tracing where AI data comes from and how it is gathered
modules.description: Learners map the landscape of data sources used to build AI systems, from public datasets and web scraping to sensors and user-generated content. They evaluate the trade-offs and ethical considerations involved in different collection methods, and assess what questions to ask about any dataset's origins.

modules.title: Privacy and Data Rights
modules.sub: Knowing your rights and responsibilities in a data-driven world
modules.description: Learners explore the legal and ethical frameworks that govern personal data, including key regulations such as GDPR, and what rights individuals hold as data subjects. They apply this knowledge to realistic scenarios, deciding how to respond to data requests, consent forms, and potential violations they might encounter in professional life.

synopsis: Develop essential data skills needed to understand how AI systems work. Learn about data quality, sources, and your rights as a data subject.
